title: Neanotis montholonii
intro: Neanotis montholonii, also known as the Indian snakewort, is a perennial plant species belonging to the family Rubiaceae. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities that have been shown to aid in the treatment of various health conditions such as fever, sore throats, and skin irritations. The bioactive constituents of Neanotis montholonii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditional herbal preparations made with Neanotis montholonii include decoctions, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to treat respiratory and skin-related ailments.
taxonomy: Neanotis montholonii is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It belongs to the genus Neanotis and is a part of the Rubiales order. The Neanotis genus is further divided into several species, with Neanotis montholonii being one of them. The Rubiaceae family is a large and diverse group of plants, commonly known as madder or bedstraw family. Neanotis montholonii is native to certain regions and is typically found in specific habitats.
